perturbation.original_text: 10. DEFAULT If either party hereto fails to perform an obligation under this Agreement, the other party, may issue a default termination letter to the party in default of an obligation under this Agreement to cure the default condition. If the default condition is not remedied within ten (10) calendar days from the day of receipt of such letter, the issuer of the default termination letter may the without the necessity of any further notice, discontinue performance and terminate this Agreement for default and pursue any other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. DEFAULT If either party hereto fails to perform an obligation under this Agreement, the other party, may issue a default termination letter to the party in default of an obligation under this Agreement to cure the default condition. If the default condition is not remedied within thirty (30) calendar days from the day of receipt of such letter, the issuer of the default termination letter may the without the necessity of any further notice, discontinue performance and terminate this Agreement for default and pursue any other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by extending the cure period for a default condition from 'ten (10) calendar days' to 'thirty (30) calendar days'. This creates an ambiguity and in-text contradiction within article 10, regarding the timeframe a default must be adressed, creating a conflict with the enforceability of the original cure period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either party hereto fails to perform an obligation under this Agreement, the other party, may issue a default termination letter to the party in default of an obligation under this Agreement to cure the default condition. If the default condition is not remedied within thirty (30) calendar days from the day of receipt of such letter, the issuer of the default termination letter may the without the necessity of any further notice, discontinue performance and terminate this Agreement for default and pursue any other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The change alters the cure period for a default condition from 'ten (10) calendar days' to 'thirty (30) calendar days'. The contradicted text also states the cure period as 'thirty (30) calendar days', this does not resolve the issue. The perturbation explanation clearly states that this creates an ambiguity and in-text contradiction, regarding the timeframe a default must be addressed, creating a conflict within article 10. Since both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text contain the same text, the change introduces an inconsistency and aligns with the definition provided for 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 15. TERMINATION (a ) Insolvency Termination - Unless otherwise waived by BUYER in writing, this Agreement shall automatically terminate if SELLER(i) becomes insolvent, (ii) suffers the appointment of receiver or trustee to manage its business operations, (iii) attempts an assignment or transfer of this Agreement or any rights conferred under this Agreement without BUYER's consent, (iv) commences any bankruptcy, reorganization or liquidation action or (v) admits in writing that it is unable to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. TERMINATION (a ) Insolvency Termination - Unless otherwise waived by BUYER in writing, this Agreement shall automatically terminate if SELLER(i) becomes insolvent, (ii) suffers the appointment of receiver or trustee to manage its business operations, (iii) attempts an assignment or transfer of this Agreement or any rights conferred under this Agreement with BUYER's consent, (iv) commences any bankruptcy, reorganization or liquidation action or (v) admits in writing that it is unable to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by removing the word 'without' from condition (iii). Now, the termination clause states that the agreement terminates if the seller attempts to transfer the agreement WITH the buyer's consent. This conflicts the intention of the term and creates a loophole in the enforceability. The conflict appears directly in the modified clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. TERMINATION (a ) Insolvency Termination - Unless otherwise waived by BUYER in writing, this Agreement shall automatically terminate if SELLER(i) becomes insolvent, (ii) suffers the appointment of receiver or trustee to manage its business operations, (iii) attempts an assignment or transfer of this Agreement or any rights conferred under this Agreement with BUYER's consent, (iv) commences any bankruptcy, reorganization or liquidation action or (v) admits in writing that it is unable to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 15(a)(iii) removes 'without', so that the agreement terminates if the seller attempts an assignment or transfer of the agreement WITH the buyer's consent. This contradicts the purpose of the clause, which is to prevent unauthorized transfers. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, thus, the contradiction is present and clear. Because the edited clause nullifies the intention, this leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 20. RELATIONSHIP OF THE PARTIES The relationship of the parties to this Agreement is that of a prime contractor and a subcontractor, and nothing herein shall be deemed or construed to create a joint venture, partnership or agency relationship between the parties for any purpose. It is further understood that each party is an independent contractor and as such shall have no authority to bind or commit the other. SELLER is expressly prohibited from communicating with Government personnel with respect to any aspect of the Project under this Agreement without the prior consent of the BUYER, or as otherwise agreed by the parties. Any authorized communications between SELLER's personnel and Government personnel shall be conducted in the presence of the BUYER's Project Manager or other authorized representative unless otherwise agreed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 20. RELATIONSHIP OF THE PARTIES The relationship of the parties to this Agreement is that of a prime contractor and a subcontractor, and nothing herein shall be deemed or construed to create a joint venture, partnership or agency relationship between the parties for any purpose. It is further understood that each party is an independent contractor and as such shall have no authority to bind or commit the other. SELLER is expressly authorized from communicating with Government personnel with respect to any aspect of the Project under this Agreement without the prior consent of the BUYER, or as otherwise agreed by the parties. Any authorized communications between SELLER's personnel and Government personnel shall be conducted in the presence of the BUYER's Project Manager or other authorized representative unless otherwise agreed by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a critical in-text contradiction by switching the word 'prohibited' to 'authorized' in Section 20. This fundamentally alters the seller's ability to communicate with Government personnel, creating ambiguity about the rules for communication. Now, the buyer can argue the term is authorized from communicating with Government Personnel, but the term "without the prior consent of the BUYER" creates the in-text contradiction about the power dynamic.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The relationship of the parties to this Agreement is that of a prime contractor and a subcontractor, and nothing herein shall be deemed or construed to create a joint venture, partnership or agency relationship between the parties for any purpose. It is further understood that each party is an independent contractor and as such shall have no authority to bind or commit the other. SELLER is expressly authorized from communicating with Government personnel with respect to any aspect of the Project under this Agreement without the prior consent of the BUYER, or as otherwise agreed by the parties. Any authorized communications between SELLER's personnel and Government personnel shall be conducted in the presence of the BUYER's Project Manager or other authorized representative unless otherwise agreed by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'prohibited' with 'authorized', fundamentally altering the seller's ability to communicate with Government personnel. This creates an in-text contradiction because the text now states the seller is 'expressly authorized from communicating with Government personnel with respect to any aspect of the Project under this Agreement without the prior consent of the BUYER'. The phrase 'without prior consent' contradicts the authorization, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about the communication rules and creates an in-text contradiction as defined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
